author: @omegafile
text:
Ya it's real.... done with 2 speakers facing in opposite directions sharing the same center and a microphone... and runs on it's own feedback.. can get a terrawatt easy. No battery or coil or magnet or power source is required... and no moving parts. Both the microphone and speakers are transducers.. meaning they are both microphones and speakers... and having a range of below 1 hertz to 20 million hertz it will pick up every vibration in an 800 mile radius. I can show you how it's done.. but it's a trillion watts AC... very dangerous. The speaker facing down picks up the vibration of the earth and the speaker facing up picks up the white noise radio waves from space. There is a tweeter cup sitting on the speaker facing up and it sits just under an air hole of a drum box and water condenses in the cup and spills over onto the speakers and starts to spin and light up like mini suns spinning like a galaxy in a box. It's so freaking easy to do!!!.. no joke. There are only 12 parts.... 2 granite piezoelectric speakers... one cog to separate the speakers at the backs so they don't touch each other... a 500 lb xylophone key mounted on a fret at the nodes tuned to 90 hertz ... a drum box tuned to 90 hertz.. a tweeter cup tuned to 100khz... 2 heavy duty tuning forks tuned to 90 hertz that go on each side of the xylophone key.... 2 mercy seats that have a fret on top.. the one facing down does not have a fret and doesn't need it.... 2 wind chimes tuned to 90 hertz mounted at the nodes on each side of the drum box.... and a wire from the positive tweeter.. the rest of the device is negative everywhere. The 2 speakers have a hole in the center and so the xylophone key bounces up and down on the fret pushing and pulling a wave out of the hole creating a doughnut field.. and so it levitates and makes it's own air and water and gravity.. simple as hell. The water bouncing in and out of the hole creates the magnetic field.. and the spinning water droplets that look like mini suns are spinning counter clockwise over the speaker facing up and clockwise over the speaker facing down.. therefore you don't need a coil.
Having a galaxy in a box powering your ship, you can travel faster than light speed... and it doesn't cost a penny because it will use the same water over and over again and you will never run out... in fact it makes it's own air, water and gravity. I figured this out 40 years ago when I was about 20 years old. First you hear the sound.. and then you see the light.... and the light is so bright that you can't even see your own feet. or the walls or anything but pure white light.

author: @stuffoflardohfortheloveof
text: Hmmm, right up my street Robert in regard to simply giving ourselves a nudge away from dream thinking to practical thinking. I assume we’d all like to believe that free energy (in its purest sense) exists. I would also suggest that anyone who finds themselves watching this video has dreamt about inventing something utilising perpetual motion etc etc blah blah. I’m just a simple dreamer in that I spend more time day dreaming than doing ‘stuff’ and so life is slowly passing me by.....anyway, I’ve recently sort of changed my perspective on ‘all of this stuff’ in that surely the solutions are staring us in the face. Hasn’t God (and I use ‘God’ here simply as a suitable noun) already provided us with several forms of free energy? The best example being the cycle of rain, gravity and evaporation.....why don’t we stop worrying about efficiencies and just utilise MORE of the ‘free’ use of a falling weight of water. Gravity is a given......the guys that ‘invented’ that gravity powered led light for 3rd world communities have it sussed in that they just got on with what we already have. Thanks Rob, a good vid that reminded me of my current state of thinking. Bloody hell I’m sounding old (only 55 ☹️) 👍

author: @SciFiFactory
text:
So this definition can mean everything or nothing. Everything can be viewed as free energy, but actually, nothing is. Sorry, in my point of view such a "definition" this is pretty useless. There already are definitions that describe more precisely what you are talking about: green energy, renewable energy, energy harvesting ....... why try to redefine a word that is synonymous for conspiracy theorists and those guys that try to build (actual) Perpetuum mobiles or whatever? 
I guess your point would be that a new word and a little-shifted viewpoint might open new ways of thinking. But I cannot even agree on this level. Because the vast majority of our society does already have this point of view. It's people that think that the energy from their walls or in petrol or coal is free that inhibit progress. And it's people that see the system as a whole that try to find beneficial and efficient solutions. (Well you might be the exception, obviously.) I have worked at an institute for solar cell research and no-one there would call it free energy. It has just a different meaning in our society. Trying to use it nonetheless is inefficient communication and thereby wastes energy. Why? You had to make a video to explain yourself...
You are a smart guy and I love your work by the way. So, this comment isn't even necessarily directed at you but mainly for those that say this explanation is brilliant. 
But it's like saying "glass wall" instead of "window" ... it might make sense in a literal point of view. But people will associate it with something else than what you are talking about. I don't see the point of trying to redefine language. At least not when we already have multiple good ways of describing the same thing. It might be worth it for things like gender equality or other kinds of biases ... but I don't see the advantage of doing this for free energy. If there is one, I think this should have been the focus of this video.
.... now I'll go and buy some working ink ...

replies.author: @SciFiFactory
replies.text:
An additional thought: 
Some people might say this opinion of mine is ignorant and narrow-minded. That I am putting things in boxes or whatever. Well yes, that's how language works. Language is an evolving and inherently democratic process. There is no single entity that defines language. It is defined by society. Everyone has a vote. So I would argue that ignoring the vote of (what seems to me like at least) 95% of the scientific community, is at least as narrow-minded as me saying that calling it free energy is inefficient communication.
:)

author: @BillDeWitt
text:
A point not addressed in the concept of "free" is initial capital. The energy returned form a crystal radio is probably 100 times less than the energy needed to mine the copper and crystal, manufacture them into usable parts and of course the energy put into making headphones. Those headphones will probably need replacing before you get back your mining energy. So even this "free" energy, which should really be called stolen energy, doesn't turn out to be free. 
But I think your main point is valid and hopefully we can create some structures that actually return more stolen energy than we put into them.

author: @kornshadow097
text:
I can summarize what most people mean by  "free energy".
 "Does it cost the end user money to utilize the energy source? " if yes... Then it's not free energy... If no, then it's free energy. 

Solar is an example since we don't have to pay for the sun's seemingly endless supply of radiating energy. 

Good argument though.

author: @agentbertram4769
text:
We need to be very careful about the definition of Free Energy.
Like you say, you have to look at the entire system as the energy has to come from somewhere.
I look at it this way.  After the set-up energy has been recouped (the set up cost if you like), then, ignoring maintenance, the resultant energy is Free.  
Next, people bang on about Zero Point energy.  Good luck with that.  
Then I hear about Over Unity energy.  I've yet to see that too.  Again, it depends upon the point of reference.
You are right Rob. It all depends upon the viewpoint or the scope of interest.
In this world, only one thing is certain. There is no such thing as a free lunch.

author: @andrewballantine
text:
Robert,

As ever your video is thought provoking and thinking keeps us alive.

As you say the universe is full of energy of all sorts, some known and many as yet undiscovered let alone understood.

Mankind does not create energy; we just harvest it to suit our needs. The miller in a wet and hilly area noticed that water poured of the hillside in an almost unstoppable manner and thought to himself, h'mm, I could use that to drive a millstone. Whereas the miller in Holland saw that water was of no use in a essentially flat country, but found the wind an excellent source of "free" energy. So, throughout history mankind has found more and more ways of harvesting energy. Every new way of harvesting energy has been poopooed until demonstrated in a convincing manner.

My favourite bit of nonsense in this respect was the belief that anyone riding on the newly invented steam train would suffocate if the train travelled at more than 15 mph yet a galloping horse is capable of 30 mph and horse riders did not suffocate.

I always surprises me how many people loose sight of the fact that all our fossil fuels are just stored solar energy. They are essentially "free" energy, in that the sun's energy is "free" to the planet. It is just that the powers-that-be decided that a small group of people should be granted a license to extract these resources and charge whatever they liked for their services, but that's another discussion altogether.

Even the sun does not "make" energy; it just rearranges the existing energy.

So I am open to any new ideas for harvesting available energy.

Thank you for provoking me sufficiently to share my thoughts.

replies.author: @andyjones7121
replies.text: I think free energy is a poor term. Free relative to what? To me, it's a matter of identifying the source. Magnet motors, gravity wheels, and (in my humble opinion) ZPE is all fantasy. In other words, can you show me where the energy is coming from? I'm a huge fan of Tesla, but the most amazing thing about him is that he didn't even know how electricity worked! He said the electron didn't exist, and referee to capacitors and coils "disrupting the ether" Or affecting the "wheelwork of nature". Just because he preferred to the "ambient medium" I don't think he was promoting ZPE, radiant energy, or some other unidentified mysterious force. He was referring to voltage differences between earth and the atmosphere, ambient heat, pressure and temperature differences, etc. According to this definition of free energy,  it's anything except pedalling a bike to turn a light on. And if you have a friend pedal, then it is free energy. Free energy to me refers to the 1000s of fake OU devices on YouTube. And no, I'm not a believer.

author: @Khwartz
text:
Sorry, I liked much your videos but here you make a little mistake: I am trained in office engineering for heat pumps and studied further for energetic engineering and COP is not exactly what you state. A COP is the ratio of usable power / COSTLY power; nothing less, nothing more.

Regards.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thanks for that - though i always understood it to be Q/W where Q is is the heat supplied to or removed from the reservoir and W is is the work consumed by the heat pump - but maybe i got it wrong

replies.author: @Khwartz
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith 

No, it is of course related but not "necessary" the same. 

Q anyway is the heat we provide, if you use this ratio "Q/W", and it is NOT the same than the energy "taken in the reservoir"; why? Because the normally heat loses of the compressor are for most of the part used and added to, what we should label "Q-out". So "Q-out" always > "Q-in". 

But, COP is here for FINANCIAL reason fondamentally, this is what everybody non-professional in the domain miss. E.g., win-turbines, solar panels, hydrau-electric power plants are all of INFINITE COP. 

Regarding what is "Free Energy", I would say that partial "free energy systems" are all those of COP ] 1.00 ; infinite [, and true ones when COP = infinite. But is indeed only based on the viewpoint of THE ENERGY COST, while we use in engineering other kind of "COPs" which take care of the costs of the installation, of the financial of the maintenance, reduced to the year and kWh produced. 

This last is for me THE REAL ONE, the real interesting and final COP; why again? Because it permits us to really compare different systems respect to the financial point of view: few systems could be cheap to construct, use, finance and having poor overunity COP but still good for poor people, and for those who has financial means, skills for, etc., more costly systems at the beginning may be much more interesting in the long run and in term of $/kWh/year (you know solar panels and wins turbines doesn't last for ever and need maintenance in industrial power plants... but heat pumps too, even if not infinite COP.). 

Of course, COPs has few to have with "PHYSICAL EFFICIENCY" which are, theoretically, ALWAYS underunity at the scale of the universe but may be considered overunity if we restrain our point of view to a specific part of the universe (as you have very well illustrates in your vid).

Wishing it could help.

author: @polosims

author: @tdsdave
text:
Fine accessing available ambient free energy sources, your not a proponent of the vast amount of quackery associated with this label that is out there.

The problem is with the label, free energy, which much as you would probably dislike is owned principally , at least on the web, by quacks who claim to have produced or knowledge of working solutions for over unity or tapping into vacuum energy.etc 

Even if you presented such a claim, my skepticism would rightfully be based upon the conservation laws, and I think that is a good thing. Your derision, probably to strong a word for it, for a default position from that level at understanding is misplaced imo. 

I and I think most people capable of comprehending the conservation laws are not blind to the fact that if a method was found to access such esoteric "free" sources that would be wonderful. However that wonder can and is exploited by snake oils sales men, so some intellectual defense to unproven claims should be encouraged not discouraged.

Good video.  Be happy to shake the hand your not using to hold the Nobel :)
All the best,
another kent person.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Essentially I think you are right I also think this is a huge topic for discussion and my 'derisive' position is mostly as a reaction to the equally derisive position of the opposite view Personally I hate being told I can't do something and just to accept I can't. I kind of have to find out for myself. But then I am also aware of the difference between the fantasist and the explorer. Anyway - you certainly made me think - cheers - Rob

replies.author: @tdsdave
replies.text:
I can imagine for persons such as yourself with a legitimate pursuit in this subject, the muddy waters created by quacks in this subject, has a real impact upon how your ideas are viewed by laymen( I'd have to label myself in this category ). For what its worth however I can tell the difference, and I wish you success in this pursuit.

Watching some of your other videos and really enjoying them.

Thanks , have a good one.

author: @brixomatic
text: I tend to agree with your logic, but I think it doesn't help. Usually we don't think of "wind", "light" or radio waves as "free energy", as we know well enough what it is and how it works, while those "free energy devices" that you can find all over the net do have a lot of quackery about it. People claiming to harvest "Quantum Energy", measuring radio waves, the heat of their own hands, light, static, the neighbour's microwave-oven, chemical reactions and whatnot, it may very well be "free energy" in the sense that they don't know where the hell it comes from, but it's certainly not tapping directly into the "quantum foam" or whatever they may think or claim they're doing. I mean I could could build a magnetic compass, displace the needle and measure the pressure of it trying to turn north and call that "free energy" machine, and claim that damn thing would change the world forever, but that'd be equally ridiculous. Usually "free energy" means: "I don't know what the hell I'm doing here, but something moves or gives a read" and that pretty much is the definition of quackery.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Lol good point lol - I think the thing here is that this is just a snippet of a mind set I posses so in isolation I agree it's not that helpful. But, I think taken together with the rational of the channel, other talking head videos I have done and the practical work I do it begins to build a picture of what I think is responsible science. Maybe I should do a kind of overview talking head video

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Hiya Mate,

To be honest - i don't really know. I just have my own way of trying to understand what is going on. Thinking of electrons as purely particles probably isn't the correct way of thinking about them - they are quite probably complex phenomena that are only partially described as particles - which means that is a useful way to look at them when thinking about certain systems - but it isn't the only way to look at them and you can take the view of them as EM waveforms. It's quite likely the true story is something we haven't got to yet. Anyway, if you look at them as waveforms they don't 'go' anywhere per se. In the same way that if you dip your finger in and out of a bowl of water you will set up a traveling wave but your finger doesn't 'go' anywhere. The wave is set up by the energy of the motion of your finger and it is that energy that 'travels' - I am not sure if this helps but it's more in line with the way i see things if that's any good.

Rob

author: @JustinHallPlus
text: I always figured that this was what you meant.  I don't believe in "free" energy in the way that Hutchinson guy does, or the way those people that try to build magnet motors and starship coils do (although the geometry of the electromagnetic fields those weird coils produce might make them useful as an antenna or general inductor, who knows), but I do think systems could be invented that could absorb the energy of virtual quantum particles that pop in and out of existence.  Just as a thought experiment, if there was a volume of space that didn't experience that random energy permeation, then there would be a potential difference between it and the rest of space, even if it contained "regular" matter, and energy could theoretically be tapped from it.  I don't know if it will ever be technologically possible to do so, but I can definitely see it.
